As at the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital consists of 387,491,560 ordinary shares of £0.01 each (the "

"), each carrying the right to one vote at a general meeting of the Company. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 387,491,560.
The figure in the preceding sentence may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Ordinary Shares under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Trustpilot and Shopify Strengthen Partnership
latest.content:
29 Jun 2026 09:00
RNS Number : 0298K
Trustpilot Group PLC
29 June 2026
Trustpilot Group PlcTrustpilot and Shopify strengthen partnership to power the future of reviews in the age of AI
LONDON - 29 June, 2026
-
Trustpilot
, the world's largest open customer feedback platform, today announces that it has become a key product partner for reviews within
Shopify
, deepening its role within one of the world's largest providers of essential internet infrastructure for commerce.
The partnership comes as trusted third-party feedback plays an increasingly important role in online shopping. As AI continues to reshape digital commerce and make it easier to launch and scale online storefronts, consumers are placing greater value on real customer experiences and independent reputation signals when deciding where to spend their money.
Through the enhanced
Trustpilot app
on the Shopify App Store, Shopify merchants will be able to connect their stores directly with the Trustpilot platform, making it easier to collect, manage, and showcase reviews.
Trustpilot's enhanced role within the Shopify ecosystem supports merchants in building a stronger foundation of customer feedback that can help reinforce transparency, credibility and consumer confidence in the age of AI-driven commerce. As AI-powered search, recommendations, and shopping assistants increasingly rely on trusted third-party signals to evaluate and surface brands, Trustpilot helps merchants strengthen the trust signals, citations, and reputation data that can influence visibility.
Adrian Blair, CEO of Trustpilot,
said
:
"Trust has become one of the defining signals people rely on when deciding which business, service or retailer to choose. As customer journeys become faster, more automated and increasingly AI-driven, people want confidence that the companies they buy from are genuine, transparent and reliable. This partnership brings Trustpilot closer to where purchase decisions are being made every day, making it easier for merchants to build credibility through open customer feedback, while helping shoppers see trusted reviews more naturally within the Shopify experience."
Deann Evans, Managing Director, EMEA of Shopify
, said: "Merchants want simple ways to build credibility with customers as they grow online. As AI changes how people discover and decide what to buy, independent customer feedback becomes one of the strongest signals a merchant can build. Making Trustpilot reviews easier to collect, manage and surface across Shopify helps our merchants compete on one of the things that matters most to any business, genuine customer trust."
The enhanced app for Shopify includes improved data synchronisation and automated features designed to simplify review collection and management for merchants. General availability of the new app is live as of June 29, 2026.
ENDS
Contacts
Trustpilot Group plc
Louise Bryant, Head of Investor Relations
Priyal Soni, Investor Relations
investor.relations@trustpilot.com
+44 (0) 7813 210 809
+44 (0) 7391 859 625
Headland Consultancy
Stephen Malthouse / Rob Walker / Charlie Pepper
trustpilot@headlandconsultancy.com
+44 (0) 20 3805 4822
About Trustpilot
Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever - to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial - we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.
Today, we have more than 361 million reviews and 160 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1,000 employees and we're headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.

company_30s.what_they_do: Trustpilot runs a global online review platform that lets consumers rate and review businesses and services. It also offers B2B tools for companies to collect, manage and showcase customer feedback.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The review economy is growing as consumers increasingly rely on peer opinions before making purchases. Trustpilot sits at the centre of that trend, providing a single, trusted source of consumer sentiment that is used by millions of shoppers and by brands for marketing, quality control and product development.
company_30s.current_state: Trustpilot is in a transitional phase: it has recently appointed a new CFO, completed a share buy‑back, and is actively engaging its community through education events. The company is still in a recovery mode after a period of volatility, and its share price has slipped below recent highs.

rns_narrative: The company is focusing on shareholder value – a new CFO has taken the helm, a share buy‑back has been finished, and a director has announced a sizeable personal investment. These moves signal confidence from insiders.

Trustpilot is also tightening its governance: a non‑executive director was appointed, a director declaration was filed, and a block admission application was lodged. The company is maintaining a steady stream of corporate updates rather than chasing headline news.
There is no evidence of a major product launch or regulatory change in the near term; the announcements are largely structural and governance‑related.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are waiting for a clear catalyst before committing.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the director’s £1 million purchase and the company’s commitment to a buy‑back as signs that management believes the stock is undervalued. They also highlight a predicted price target of 278p based on a technical outside‑bar model.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue that the company’s recent price drop is not yet a deep crash, that the share volume remains thin, and that without a substantive product or earnings upgrade the price could stay flat or decline further.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has been steady but low‑volume. The community has not yet moved into a frenzy, suggesting that any significant move will be driven by a new announcement rather than market sentiment.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Trustpilot is flat – no recent spikes in public interest. This silence means there is a high potential for a surprise catalyst to move the stock sharply.

near_term_catalysts: A formal earnings release or an earnings‑upgrade from a major rating agency could give the stock a push.
If the newly appointed CFO announces a capital‑structure change – for example, a new round of debt or a strategic partnership – that would signal confidence.
A volume spike above 10 % of the 20‑day average on a day the price breaks a key support level would confirm a rebound.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next two to three months include: (1) any announcement of new revenue streams or product launches; (2) a sustained increase in average daily volume; and (3) a breakout above the 200‑day moving average, which would suggest the start of a recovery trend.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a potential capitulation cycle – the price is still far from its all‑time high and has not yet hit a clear bottom. A mean‑reversion play would need a clear trigger; without one the current position is still in a waiting mode.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the evidence for a deep crash is weak and no clear catalyst is imminent.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the director’s personal investment and the buy‑back are interpreted as confidence signals, the stock could rally to the recent high of 255p, giving a ~13% upside from the current 225p level. A further rebound to 280p would produce a 25% gain.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The lack of a significant price drop means the market has not yet recognized the stock as undervalued. Without a new product launch or earnings beat, the price could stay flat or decline modestly, limiting upside potential.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 13–25% if the price recovers to 255–280p. Potential downside: the stock could fall a few percentage points if sentiment remains unchanged. The asymmetry is modest – the upside is limited because the current price is close to recent highs.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only if the share price breaks above the 200‑day moving average on volume that exceeds 10 % of its 20‑day average. Allocate 5–10 % of the portfolio, set a stop 5 % below the entry, and target 255p or 280p depending on the strength of the breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for a quarterly earnings release that shows a year‑over‑year revenue lift or a credit rating upgrade. Also monitor any announcement of a new partnership or product that could add a new revenue stream.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files a bankruptcy petition or a delisting notice, exit immediately. Otherwise, remain in a neutral stance until a clear catalyst emerges.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Trustpilot is in a cautious recovery phase with no deep crash yet; the best play is to stay alert for a clear earnings or structural catalyst that could lift the price to 255–280p, offering a modest upside while keeping downside exposure minimal.
